subject: Public Health
title: Global Health Financing
description: A micro-lesson that makes Global Health Financing usable.

hook.text: Low-income countries spend $41 per person per year on health. High-income countries spend over $4,000.

buildup.text: This 100-fold gap determines who gets vaccinated, who gets surgery, and who dies from treatable conditions.

discovery.text: International health aid—about $40 billion annually—fills some gaps but represents less than 1% of global health.

twist.text: Domestic resource mobilization—countries funding their own health systems—is more sustainable than foreign aid.

climax.text: The Abuja Declaration asked African countries to allocate 15% of budgets to health—most haven't reached it.

punchline.text: Health is a political choice. The money exists. The priorities don't.

quiz.question: How much do low-income countries spend per person per year on health?
